doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_max: return the larger of two operands per IEEE 754-2008 `maxNum` semantics — NaN propagates only when both are NaN. Signed zero is ordered `+0 > -0` via a special pre-test. Class is 'arithmetic' (50ms per-case budget). Sibling to mpfr_min — structurally identical with compare branches and signed-zero polarity flipped (tie goes to `b` instead of `a` per the C reference at mpfr/src/minmax.c L87–L90).
c_signature: int mpfr_max(mpfr_t rop, mpfr_srcptr x, mpfr_srcptr y, mpfr_rnd_t rnd)
divergence_from_c: C mutates rop and returns ternary as int; TS takes prec positional and returns {value, ternary}. Behaviour matches mpfr/src/minmax.c L65–L91 exactly. IEEE 754-2008 maxNum, not 754-2019 maximum (which would propagate NaN). Both-zero pre-test is load-bearing for the same reason as mpfr_min.
refs: mpfr/src/minmax.c L65–L91 — the C reference for mpfr_max.
src/internal/mpfr/round_raw.ts — substrate rounding for the prec-refit step.
src/core.ts — locked types; posZero / negZero factories.
CLAUDE.md 'Hallucination-risk callouts' — Signed zero is real (max(+0, -0) = +0); NaN ≠ NaN (IEEE 754-2008 maxNum); rounding-mode count is FIVE.
